primary_causes: Predominantly rice-based diet with minimal haem iron
Very high wasting rate depresses iron stores
open defecation → repeated gut infections → impaired absorption
Festival/ritual avoidance of animal foods among tribal communities
Poor PDS fortification reach in dense forest blocks
Forest foods (wild greens, tubers) limited in lean season
PDS oil fortification coverage only ~45% in tribal blocks
Child mortality from measles/diarrhoea worsened by Vit A deficiency
Phytate-rich cereal diet (mandia, bajra) inhibits zinc absorption
Very high stunting rate itself is both driver and consequence of zinc deficiency
Despite non-vegetarian traditions, very low portion sizes of meat/fish
Limited fermented food access outside local markets
Dense forest cover limits sun exposure for women and children indoors
Low fortified oil coverage

context.brief: Earns the most malnourished district tag in India with 60.6% stunting — nearly 2 in 3 children. A densely forested tribal heartland in the Jharkhand−Odisha border belt. Land alienation from mining has disrupted food sovereignty.

name: Bahraich
source: NFHS-5 (2019-21), NITI Aayog District Nutrition Profile

primary_causes: Extremely high vegetarianism (72%) with minimal egg/meat consumption
Tannin-rich chai consumed with every meal blocks iron absorption
Anemia among women (62%) drives poor foetal iron stores
Child anemia 72% — highest among the 10 districts
ASHA worker IFA distribution compliance low in border blocks
No PDS vitamin A oil fortification in most UP blocks
Lean summer season — vegetables unavailable
Vitamin A deficiency linked to 72% child anemia here
High phytate diet: roti, rice, dal without any zinc enhancers
Child stunting and zinc deficiency are bidirectionally linked
Among the most vegetarian populations in India (72%)
Very low egg consumption especially among women
No state-level B12 supplementation programme
Long winters with reduced sun hours in the Terai belt
Women's low outdoor mobility limits sun exposure

context.brief: A densely populated Terai district bordering Nepal with the highest child anemia (72%) among top-10. NFHS-4→5 shows stunting reduced from 65% to 52% — progress is real but still catastrophic by global standards.

name: Bijapur
source: NFHS-5 (2019-21), NHSRC India, NITI District Profile

primary_causes: Maoist insurgency disrupts food supply chains and PDS access
Very low institutional delivery — mothers miss IFA supplementation
Forest block villages >15km from any PDS fair price shop
Repeated malarial episodes (endemic) worsen anaemia
Only 47% institutional delivery means neonatal iron stores are poor
Extremely low PDS coverage (55%) means fortified oil barely reaches
Road inaccessibility during monsoon cuts off 30% of villages
Forest food disruption from shifting cultivation patterns
Maize-dominant diet with very high phytate:
Lack of zinc-ORS use during diarrhoea episodes
Despite non-veg tradition, security concerns limit hunting/gathering
Limited dairy access
Dense forest canopy limits sunlight for children under forest cover
No fortified oil or DFS programme reaching interior villages

context.brief: India's most insurgency-affected district makes it also the most service-deprived. 'Suposhit Bijapur' programme launched 2021 using millets, eggs and peanuts to address the 54% stunting. Road access cuts off 60+ villages in monsoon.

name: Alirajpur
source: NFHS-5 (2019-21), NITI Aayog District Nutrition Profile

primary_causes: Women's anemia (75%) is among worst in India — intergenerational malnutrition
Extremely high wasting (35%) — highest of all 10 districts
Migration for 6 months (sugarcane labour) disrupts nutrition for women
Poor ICDS Anganwadi attendance in scattered hamlets
Iron cooking vessels replaced by aluminium — loss of dietary iron
Summer migration means children miss Vitamin A supplementation doses at Anganwadis
Maize-kapas belt — limited vegetable diversity
PDS oil often diverted/does not reach tribal hamlets
Alirajpur has India's highest wasting (35%) — severe acute malnutrition ↔ zinc deficiency loop
Maize-dal diet with phytate binding
Despite non-veg tradition, seasonal migration disrupts dietary patterns
No B12 supplementation outreach
Dense hilly forest; poor oil fortification reach
Seasonal migration breaks contact with public health workers

context.brief: Alirajpur has India's highest wasting rate (35%) among the top 10 — indicating extreme acute malnutrition. Seasonal migration of Bhil families to sugarcane farms in Gujarat disrupts nutrition, school feeding and Anganwadi services for 6 months/year.

name: Nandurbar
source: NFHS-5 (2019-21), NITI Aayog District Nutrition Profile

primary_causes: Child anemia 71% despite better delivery coverage — diet is the bottleneck
Sugarcane and paddy dominance = iron-poor diet
Very high vegetable prices at tribal haat bazaars
Iron in paddy husked away during modern milling
Limited orange/yellow vegetable access at tribal markets
PDS oil vitamin A fortification underreached
Seasonal dietary monotony
High phytate from dal-rice diet without yoghurt or fermented food
Despite tribal non-veg tradition, quantity is very low
Limited egg and dairy consumption
Monsoon overcast + indoor cooking
PDS fortified oil not reaching all tribal hamlets

context.brief: Maharashtra's most malnourished district despite its proximity to a prosperous state. The paradox of high institutional delivery (82%) yet 48% stunting reveals that birth care alone is insufficient — early childhood nutrition must start at conception.
key_interventions: Rajmata Jijau Amhi Bala Vikrya Yojana

name: Sheopur
source: NFHS-5 (2019-21), NITI Aayog District Nutrition Profile

primary_causes: Sahariya tribe — India's most food insecure PVTGs (Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group)
Extreme seasonal hunger from April-June (lean period)
Women's anemia 70% — one of worst in central India
Low dal consumption due to affordability
Sparse Chambal riverine vegetation — limited green leafy vegetable access in summer
PVTGs often missed by PDS distribution
Rotational hunger during lean season creates acute zinc depletion
High infection load from Chambal river flooding
Sahariya are partial vegetarians with very limited meat access
Extreme poverty prevents regular egg/dairy purchase
Long rainy season in Chambal valley
Limited fortified food access

context.brief: Home to the Sahariya tribe — India's only PVTG (Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group) classified in MP. They faced literal starvation in 2002 despite Sheopur being near Kuno national park. Remains one of the most food-insecure districts in India.

name: Barmer
source: NFHS-5 (2019-21), NITI Aayog District Nutrition Profile

primary_causes: Child anemia 75% — among the highest in any non-tribal district in India
Extremely vegetarian (85%) with no egg/meat tradition
Desert water has high fluoride interfering with iron absorption
Chaas (buttermilk) consumed at all meals — calcium blocks iron
Guar, bajra dominant crops — phytate-rich diet
Desert environment — very limited green vegetable diversity throughout the year
PDS oil does reach but fortification level often substandard
Rajasthani dal-baati-churma diet is nutritious but vitamin A poor
High phytate bajra-dal diet; chaas inhibits zinc absorption
High infection load from unclean water
85% vegetarian; strict Jain/Rajput communities avoid even eggs
No state B12 supplementation; no fortified foods reach this area
Among the most severe B12 deficiency districts in India
Ironically — despite a desert, women are fully covered (purdah)
Limited sun exposure due to cultural norms

context.brief: A rich paradox — oil wells (Cairn India) nearby yet 75% child anemia. The Thar desert belt has India's most extreme B12 deficiency in vegetarian populations. Female literacy (33%) is among the lowest in Rajasthan, perpetuating intergenerational malnutrition.

name: Nabarangpur
source: NFHS-5 (2019-21), NITI Aayog for States Dashboard, Odisha Child Nutrition Reports

primary_causes: Underweight 47% — among worst in all districts — indicates severe food insecurity
Dependence on low-diversity rice diet depleted of micronutrients
High open defecation → gut infections → iron malabsorption
Food taboos for women restrict iron-rich foods during pregnancy
Kondh community has protein taboos restricting egg consumption for women
Pre-harvest lean season severely limits green vegetable access (Aug-Oct)
PDS oil reaches but Vitamin A fortification compliance is poor in Odisha's tribal blocks
Rice-dominant diet with poor zinc bioavailability
High wasting (25%) is co-caused by zinc deficiency
Non-vegetarian tradition but poor portion sizes
Fermented foods (kanji, rice beer) provide only trace B12
Dense forest interior; monsoon overcast
Women's limited outdoor exposure

context.brief: Nabarangpur's child underweight (47%) is among the highest in India — meaning almost half the children weigh less than normal even for their height. Over 5,000 children were reported malnourished in November 2024. The district borders the 'hunger belt' of Koraput, Malkangiri, and Rayagada.

name: Gadchiroli
source: NFHS-5 (2019-21), NITI Aayog District Profile, Maharashtra Nutrition Reports

primary_causes: Child anemia 77% — highest of all 10 districts — shockingly high even in a non-veg community
Despite improved stunting, anemia surged from 58% to 77% between NFHS-4 and NFHS-5
High wasting (30%) suggests acute severe malnutrition depressing serum iron
Malarial endemicity in Gadchiroli — malaria destroys red blood cells
Even with forest foods, absorption blocked by high infection/inflammation
Forest foods seasonal; Madia Gonds dependent on forest for nutrition year-round
Forested roads inaccessible for 5 months/year — PDS deliveries disrupted
Wasting at 30% creates zinc-growth failure loop
Maize and rice monotony with poor zinc content
Non-vegetarian but forest game access disrupted by conservation laws
Limited B12 from available food sources
Longest monsoon among the 10 districts — 7 months of cloud cover
Dense Gondwana forest limits sunlight

context.brief: Gadchiroli's anemia spiked from 58% to 77% in children between NFHS-4 and NFHS-5 — a +19 percentage point catastrophe. Despite improving stunting, anemia worsened dramatically, likely linked to surging malaria and forest-food disruption. The Madia Gond community faces extinction-level nutrition pressure.

name: Dungarpur
source: NFHS-5 (2019-21), NITI District Nutrition Profile, Rajasthan SDG Report

primary_causes: Child anemia 73% despite institutional delivery 84% and ODF status — diet is the bottleneck
Iron-inhibiting tannins in local chai with every meal
Intercropping zone — maize+bajra dominant, low iron bioavailability
Fluorosis from groundwater interacts with iron metabolism
Limited orange/yellow vegetable cultivation in hilly tribal terrain
PDS oil fortification is administrative rather than operational in Dungarpur
Typical Rajasthani tribal diet: phytate-rich with limited meat frequency
Bhil tribal — non-vegetarian in tradition but limited consumption frequency
No B12 fortification or targeted supplementation reach
Hilly terrain with forest cover; monsoon overcast 4 months
Cultural norms around women's sun exposure

context.brief: Dungarpur sits in the Vagad subregion between the Aravalli hills — a striking tribal-hill district where despite 84% institutional delivery and good ODF coverage, child anemia stubbornly sits at 73%. It's a classic case of 'system reach is fine, dietary quality is the killer'.
